streams.pump_role: single combined feed containing both substrates in DES
streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: single premixed feed essential to ensure nitrile oxide generated thermally is immediately captured by dipolarophile, preventing dimerization to furoxan. Concentration reduced from batch 0.5 M to 0.067 M based on calculation results for reactor sizing.

mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: single stream design eliminates need for mixer. Both substrates premixed in DES before entering heated coil to ensure immediate trapping of reactive nitrile oxide intermediate.
post_reactor_steps: collect product stream for offline workup with water extraction
chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition driven by in-situ nitrile oxide generation at 120°C. Key design: single premixed stream prevents nitrile oxide dimerization. Calculations show mass-transfer limitation (Da=39.4) but acceptable for this reaction class. High BPR (5 bar) required for gas-liquid system. Flow enables precise temperature control and reduced residence time (15 min batch → 0.8 min flow) with 20× intensification factor.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation estimated 0.8 min (range 0.5-2.2 min) with 20× intensification factor. Analogy 1 used 5 min for photocycloaddition but different mechanism. Using calculation result as it's specific to thermal cycloaddition kinetics.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation result: 0.5 mL/min total flow rate to achieve target residence time in 0.38 mL reactor volume. Verified: τ = V/Q = 0.38/0.5 = 0.76 min ≈ 0.8 min.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch (120°C) - Chemistry Plan confirms thermal activation required for nitrile oxide generation. Analogies used higher temps (154°C, 200°C) but for different reaction types.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Calculation result: 0.067 M in reactor (diluted from batch 0.5 M for proper flow dynamics). Analogy 1 used 0.5 M but different solvent system.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation: BPR REQUIRED at 5.0 bar for gas-liquid system to maintain gas solubility and prevent uncontrolled degassing. Analogy 2 used 47.6 bar but for different solvent system.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_type: Chemistry Plan specifies coil reactor for thermal reaction. Consistent with Analogy 1 (steel coil) and calculation assumptions.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: Stainless steel appropriate for 120°C thermal reaction. No photochemistry so transparency not needed. Analogy 1 used steel for high-temp operation.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Calculation used 1.0 mm ID for proper heat transfer and flow dynamics (Re=10.6, laminar). Analogy 3 used 1.2 mm for similar thermal process.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation result: 0.38 mL volume for 1.0 mm ID, 0.48 m length coil to achieve target residence time.
reasoning_per_field.wavelength_nm: Chemistry Plan confirms no photochemical activation - purely thermal reaction. Set to null as no light source needed.
reasoning_per_field.deoxygenation_method: Chemistry Plan confirms not oxygen-sensitive, proceeds under air. Set to null as no deoxygenation required.
